Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

is our personality is an illusion? I, Dayal Nitai, we will not exist if he goes out of this material

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2010-09-27 · 8 answers
is our personality is an illusion? I, Dayal Nitai, we will not exist if he goes out of this material world? i mean my sufferings, my joys are illusions without purpose?
user [38] · 2010-09-27
Personality is real, upadhis are illusion (noneternal). Sarvopadhi vinirmuktam...
user [154] · 2010-09-28
Your sufferings are an illusion, and the joys that are actually sufferings are also an illusion, they do not constitute you and to the degree you define yourself in these terms you are in illusion about who you are.
user [552] · 2010-09-28
Without purpose? I, Dayal Nitai, who looks for and suffer to find the true, am an illusion?
user [154] · 2010-09-28
" I, Dayal Nitai, who looks for and suffer to find the true" -- you, the I, the self, the soul is not an illusion. Youre the soul. It is also not clear what means "uffer to find the true" did you mean uffer to find the truth?
user [154] · 2010-09-28
In fact the activities, the material world and karma is not an illusion, but to identify and to desire the fruits that they bring is an illusion.
user [447] · 2010-09-29
It seems to me that the OPs concern is in roundabout this:

If all our sufferings and all our joys are taken away from us, then is there anything left? Do we even exist apart from our sufferings and joys?
What constitutes human personality? How does human personality exist?
What is the soul? How is one soul different from another? Is each soul unique? If yes, how, in what way?
user [154] · 2010-09-30
Excellency questions Baker. Answers are in the Bhagavatam from chapter 1 to... let me see. But each can be a separate question I hope. In essence -- if upadis are removed from a conditioned soul it has nothing to hold on to, liberated soul has no upadis, and all his feelings are real.
user [447] · 2010-09-30
Suhotra Swami wrote a book on the topic: Transcendental Personalism

http://www.suhotraswami.net/library/Transcendental_Personalism.pdf
